grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements row 232; paper failure mode domestic_violence

scenarios.opening_query: My kids' other parent has a new partner with a past and I'm losing sleep over the overnights.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The new partner is a registered offender whose conditions prohibit contact with minors — I reported it, and DHS opened a case on the household this week.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The new partner is a registered offender barred from contact with minors — I reported it and DHS opened a case this week.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether registry conditions and an agency case exist
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'past' is an old DUI — nothing child-related, no agency involved; I want the parenting plan to add a no-drinking-during-parenting-time clause.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's an old DUI, nothing child-related — I just want a no-drinking clause in the plan.
scenarios.vetting_note: Registry-condition fact vs ordinary new-partner anxiety.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex moved the kids to her mother's without telling me and school pickup was chaos today.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She moved because I learned her boyfriend put hands on my son — I'm not fighting the move; I want protection for the kids from HIM, and she'll cooperate.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She moved because her boyfriend put hands on my son — I don't oppose the move. I want protection for the kids from him, and she'll cooperate.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether protection from a third party, not the move, is the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no safety issue — she moved for a job interview week and blew up the schedule; I want the plan's relocation terms enforced.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No safety issue — she moved for a job week and blew up the schedule. Enforce the relocation terms.

scenarios.opening_query: I need emergency custody of my grandkids before something terrible happens in that house.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues || Family Law > Grandparent/Third Party Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: DHS investigated twice and closed both times as unfounded — my path now is a third-party custody petition with my own evidence, not the agency.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: DHS investigated twice and closed it both times — so it's a third-party custody petition on my own evidence now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the agency route is exhausted leaving a private petition
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: DHS just removed the kids Friday — placement review is Tuesday and I want them placed with me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: DHS removed them Friday — placement review is Tuesday and I want them with me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Agency-closed vs agency-active postures for the same fear.

scenarios.opening_query: The other parent keeps our daughter's passport locked up and vacation is in three weeks.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'vacation' fight is cover — he's told two people he plans to take her to his home country permanently, and his parents there have enrolled her in school.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's not really about vacation — he's told people he plans to take her to his home country for good, and his parents enrolled her in school there.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether abduction risk with foreign ties is the real issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No abduction risk — he's just controlling about documents; the parenting plan needs a passport-handling clause.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No abduction fear — he's just controlling. The plan needs a passport clause.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pre-abduction indicators route to international family law.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's school keeps calling me instead of his mother even on her weeks, and it's causing fights.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Parental Responsibility
scenarios.hidden_fact: The school calls me because on her weeks he's been caught twice vandalizing lockers — the district is now billing us for damages, and that liability question is what I need handled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They call because he's vandalized lockers twice on her weeks — the district is billing us for damages, and that liability is my question.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether parental liability for the child's acts is the issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No damage bills — the calls are a contact-list mix-up plus a mom who won't answer, and the plan's communication terms need fixing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No damages — it's a contact-list mix-up and unanswered calls. Fix the plan's communication terms.
scenarios.vetting_note: Parental-liability billing selects the dedicated label.

scenarios.opening_query: My teen daughter told me something about her stepfather that I can't unhear, and her mother calls it a lie.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || General Litigation > Child Abuse Victims || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: Police confirmed the abuse and he's been charged — beyond custody, I want a civil case for my daughter as the victim, for her therapy and future.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Police confirmed it and he's charged — beyond custody, I want the civil victim's case for her therapy and future.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a confirmed abuse case supports a civil victim claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The investigation just opened — DHS and police are interviewing this week, and custody has to respond to the open case first.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The investigation just opened — DHS and police interviews are this week, so it's the open case first.
scenarios.vetting_note: Confirmed-vs-investigating posture; both reroute away from plain custody.

scenarios.opening_query: Our custody exchange spot doesn't feel safe anymore and my ex refuses to change it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's not my ex — his new roommate shows up to exchanges uninvited, follows my car out, and has left notes on my windshield; I need protection from the roommate, who's a stranger to me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's his roommate — shows up uninvited, follows my car, leaves windshield notes. I need protection from him; he's a stranger to me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: who the threatening person is and their relationship to the user
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing threatening — the gas station just got sketchy at night, and the plan needs a new exchange location clause.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing threatening — the spot's just sketchy at night. The plan needs a new location.
scenarios.vetting_note: Third-party stalking inside a custody logistics frame.

scenarios.opening_query: I just learned my ex has been recording every phone call I make to my children.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's also recording MY end of calls with my lawyer when the kids hand me over — our state requires all-party consent, and I want the interception itself pursued.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's even caught my calls with my lawyer at exchanges — our state is all-party consent, and I want the interception itself pursued.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether unlawful interception is an independent claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just the kids' calls on his own phone — creepy but likely legal; what I want is the plan's phone-privacy terms tightened.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the kids' calls on his own phone — creepy, but I mainly want the plan's phone terms tightened.
scenarios.vetting_note: Consent-statute fact separates a privacy tort from plan modification.
